feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Master Data and Price Procedure from Sales order to invoice
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

I have a question about the OTC flow (Sales Order → Delivery → Invoice) and how pricing behaves when copy control is set to not re-determine prices during invoice creation.

In this setup, the system simply copies the pricing data from the sales order — even if some master data changes between the delivery and invoice steps, including condition types that depend on that master data.

One workaround I'm thinking of: manually update the master data on the sales order and recalculate pricing (VA02), then update the relevant field on the delivery (VL02N) if it exists there — and then create a fresh invoice with the updated data.

My question is: if the delivery is already completed/goods issued, can doing this process cause any issues or impact in other steps of the flow?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: update open delivery quantity even if a delivery block is set
entry.content:
Hi,

The business is asking to update open credit values even if a delivery block was set on schedule line of a sales order.

OSS note "741980 - Open credit values with delivery block in the sales order " does not meet business requierments. 

How can I change this standard behavior?

entry.title: Fix delivery dates for production in Sales Scheduling Agreements within frozen zone
entry.content:
My customer is using Sales Scheduling agreements, but is struggling with the integration to Production Planning and Procurement. When we create the SSA and enter the delivery schedule it will generate requirement that are supposed to be fixed when they are within a number of days within the delivery date. This "frozen zone" is about 30 days. How can we make sure that schedule lines with a date within the frozen zone cannot be changed? We are using the production go-ahead period, but that does not help us anywhere because it seems only for informational purposes. 

We are using SSA with delivery schedules and SSA with delivery orders (MAIS).

entry.title: Error message SIPT805 - " Total value is higher than in original invoice"
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

I am experiencing a problem when creating a credit note linked to an invoice in SAP SD.

When I try to save/post the document, the system displays an alert stating that the credit note amount exceeds the original invoice amount, and when I save it, no accounting document is generated and the credit note does not appear in VBRK or SIPT_VBRK.

However, upon checking in detail, the amounts appear to be exactly the same as those on the invoice.

Has anyone else experienced a similar issue?

Any suggestions on what else I should check would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: From where the payment method is getting fetched in sales order line item?
entry.content: I need to understand from where the payment method is getting fetched in sales order line item, as customer master data is having 'blank' payment method but still somehow in sales order line-item payment method 'direct debit' is getting picked automatically. I need to know which configuration is bringing this. because of this the invoice is getting created with 'direct debit' payment method & payment is also happening with 'direct debit' method, which doesn't need. Hence, I need to rectify the config and correct asap.

entry.title: Down Payment adjustment for Partial delivery/Partial invoice
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The customer is on SAP S/4 HANA On Premise solution- SAP S/4 HANA 2023-FPS 1

We have implemented Scope item BKJ- Sales order processing with Customer Down Payment. Now the requirement is to have the down payment adjusted for Partial invoices created against sales order line item. 

Scenario as below.

Order quantity - 100 EA. Delivery to be split on multiple delivery dates on the sales order.

Price per unit - 50 USD/ EA

Total order value - 5000 USD

Advance received - 500 USD (10% of order value)

Partial delivery/ invoice of 10 EA should adjust this 10% advance at the invoice and show an outstanding of only 450 USD against the total invoice value of 500 USD

Is there any Standard SAP solution for the On premise version 2023? 

I know that we can make use of the combination of Service material and condition type to provide a custom solution for this requirement but as far as possible we would want to avoid that.

Any help or pointers would be apprciated.

Thanks in advance.

Kartik Shah

entry.title: Sales Price Master Upload
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I attempted to upload sales pricing data using the Migration Cockpit. Specifically, this upload is for the unit price using a customized condition type (ZPR1). However, the system is throwing an error stating that the condition type ZPR1 is not supported by this migration object.

Could anyone please guide me on how to resolve this issue?

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: FOREIGN TRADE DATA: Function code cannot be selected
entry.content:
Hello,

Good day!

We encountered an error in the Foreign Trade Data item. When navigating through each description in the Incompleteness Analysis, we received the error “Function code cannot be selected” and the fields were disabled. 

The invoice in error is a cancellation invoice.

Could you please assist with the possible solution?

Please check the attached screenshot for reference.

Thank you!

entry.title: While executing WB2R_SV for a Sales commission contract it is giving pricing error in SAP S4 hana
entry.content:
Hi All

In the Sandbox SAP S4 hana 2023 on premise system while executing the WB2r_SV for external Sales commission contract I am getting the below error.

Sales commission contract:

Settlement procedure

and the below is SD pricing procedure for SO and Billing

Let me know if you need any further details.

Other Information: Created the sales order, delivery and invoice and External partner also is part of the sales order and invoice.

Successfully tested the Sales Rebate testing without any error with above SD pricing procedure and relevant Rebate Settlement pricing procedure. I don't think any issues with SD pricing procedure but not sure about the Sales commission settlement procedure.

Thanks

Sri

entry.title: Batch invoicing job for delivery plus sales order with same WBS or same linked Sales order
entry.content:
Hi Members,

I have requirement to implement an automated invoicing routine that automatically identifies and uses the pair of Sales Order and Outbound to create the billing document. The routine will also update the Billing Document's output type EDI = Immediately and save it once again. 

This routine cannot take the customer PO as reference as in the past it created issues as it would combine all outbounds that were linked to the same customer PO and create a single invoice (lump sum).
This would cause rejection from our customer, as a single billing document per SO / Outbound is expected per delivery.
we need to introduce a batch invoicing job for delivery plus sales order with the same WBS element or same linked sales order for sales Org. 
 
I checked for "schedule billing creation" app but parameter on the app is not satisfied. 
 
Thanks and Regards,
Rajesh
 
.

entry.title: Correction of header data without changing item details for Poland credit memo
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a standard credit‑memo document type in Poland that permits corrections to header‑level data without altering the line‑item details, specifically for the purpose of addressing formal errors?

This clarification is required for our Poland implementation ticket, as we need to ensure compliance with local legal and accounting requirements while avoiding unnecessary changes to item‑level data.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,
Yoshinee Mootheeveeren

entry.title: unable to locate the path where billing plan type 03 is mainained from the attched screen
entry.content:
unable  to locate the path where billing plan type 03 is maintained from  the attached screen .

Kindly share the path 

i was unable to find in below 2 paths

IMG > Sales and Distribution > Billing > Payment Cards > Maintain Payment Card Plan Types.

IMG > Sales and Distribution > Billing > Billing plan  > Define billing plan .

entry.title: Restrict change of conditions in sales order depending on user status in item position
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have the requirement to restrict/block any changes of conditions in sales order items when the sales manager clears the invoicing block. 

Is it possible to solve this in customizing via status scheme (BS02)? 

E.g.:

A user status is set automatically or manually after the invoice block is cleared. The new status restricts/blocks any changes of conditions until the invoice is created. I have already looked at possible business transactions in object types "sales order header" and "sales order item" but there is no transaction type like "change of condition" or just "sales order change".

I assume there are also user exits to handle this requirement but I was wondering if there is a way in standard customizing to fulfil this requirement.

Thanks!

entry.title: Single billing document to be created with TAN & TAD item Category from a Single Sales order
entry.content:
Hi,

Hope you are doing well.

I have requirement where my sales order has 2 line items one with TAN & other with TAD item category. Now i can do delivery for Line item 10. But while doing the billing i need both line items to be copied into a single billing document. Which copy control set up i need to update to achieve this requirement.

Thanks,

Sravan

entry.title: Multiple language Description for sales documents
entry.content:
Hi,

How can i maintain multiple languages for a sales document?

For example document type IN : iNQUIRY , I need to have it for french dutch and spanish

entry.title: Requirements Class configuration (OVZH equivalent) in CBC?
entry.content:
[S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602] Where to find
Requirements Class configuration (OVZH equivalent) in CBC?

Hi SAP Community!

I'm working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 implementation and ran into a challenge related to the availability check (aATP) configuration for sales orders.

BACKGROUND
The business operates with high-volume sales orders that are split into multiple deliveries throughout the week (a process we call delivery scheduling or phasing). The problem is that when the sales order is created, the system immediately reserves the total stock quantity — even when the deliveries are planned for D+2, D+3, or D+4. This consumes the available stock balance in real time and prevents other orders from being confirmed.

SOLUTION I'M EXPLORING
My idea is to activate the availability check in the Requirements Class without activating the transfer of requirements. This way, the aATP would calculate the available balance in real time without creating a firm reservation at order creation. The stock would only be reserved when the BOP (Backorder Processing) runs and confirms the daily quantity — keeping the prioritization logic intact.

MY QUESTION
In SAP S/4HANA on-premise or Private Cloud, this configuration is done via transaction OVZH (Define Requirements Classes) under:
SPRO > Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements > Transfer of Requirements > Define Requirements Classes

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where can I find this configuration activity in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC)? I've navigated through the CBC but couldn't locate the specific activity.

Environment details:
Release: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
Configuration environment: SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC)
Module: SD — Sales and Distribution

Any help is greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Link Milestones Billing Plan to billing plan items via API
entry.content:
Dears,

the customer's need is to create a sales order via Interface including Milestone Billing Plan.

My question is about the chance to handle the Milestone Billing Plan Items via API for sales order creation.

I found:

1) https://me.sap.com/notes/3656020 Unable to link Project Milestones to Billing Plan Items via API or SAPGUI apps in which I can see it should be possible

2) Create Sales Order with Billing Plan | SAP Help Portal. Here it seems to be possible the Billing Plan Integration. The example refers to "ProposedBillingDocumentType": "FAZ" (Down Payment) but I think it's possible to handle the code for Milestone and Final Invoice F2.

Do you have evidence if it's possible or not to integrate via API the Mileston Billing Plan during Sales Order entry?

Thanks

Best regards

Sebastiano

entry.title: [S/4HANA Private Cloud] Flexible Workflow – Sales Order Remains Blocked After Auto-Skip
entry.content:
I’m facing an issue with Flexible Workflow for Sales Orders.

Requirement

  • ZCS + condition ZP06 : trigger approval
  • ZCS without ZP06 : auto-release (no approval)

Implementation

  • BAdI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON : sets approval reason Z005 for all ZCS
  • Workflow created in Manage Workflows for Sales Orders
  • Custom condition evaluator ZSWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVA

Important Constraint

  • BAdI runs before COMMIT WORK
  • Pricing data (ZP06 in PRCD_ELEMENTS) not yet persisted
  • SELECT not allowed due to transactional contract FUNCTIONAL

 Therefore, launch condition based on ZP06 is unreliable

Workaround Applied

  • Launch condition = NONE
  • Step condition = check ZP06 (via custom evaluator after COMMIT)

Behavior

  • With ZP06 : approval works 
  • Without ZP06:
    • Step condition = FALSE → step skipped 
    • Workflow log = Completed (SWI1) 
    • BUT order remains blocked 

Error

V2468 – Sales document is being processed for approval

Observation

  • No pending work items
  • Workflow WS02000006 = Completed
  • Status in VA03 = In Approval Process

Root Cause (suspected)

If step is skipped:

  • releaseSalesOrder action is never triggered
  • Approval status is not reset
  • Document remains locked

Question

How can we handle this standard gap?

  • Is there a way to force release when no approval step is executed?
  • Any BAdI / enhancement to reset approval status?
  • Or recommended design pattern for this scenario?

entry.title: App or tool for massive upload condititons material inclusion/exclusion
entry.content:
Hi All,

We need to do massive upload for conditions of material inclusion/exclusion.

Could you please inform us if there is an app or tool for this upload?

With MASS tcode can we upload?

 

Best regards,

Roberto Teodozio

entry.title: Changing the shipping point after integration with TM
entry.content:
Hello everybody, Ilia is here.

I'm looking for solution advice for a given business process and system landscape. I would be grateful to hear your comments:

Process:

We are currently in an OrderToCash process involving the following SAP modules: SD, LE, TM and EWM.
High level business process looks like this:
At regular intervals, the customer submits a request for the delivery of specific materials by a specific date. The request is processed outside SAP, but information regarding when and in what quantity the customer expects delivery is received in SAP via an interface.
Next, we need to locate the material in the warehouse, prepare it for transport and load it onto the scheduled truck.
Logistically, the process ends once the vehicle has left the company premises. Subsequent financial processes have been omitted for the sake of simplicity.

There is a logistical feature to the process:

Sometimes large quantities of materials are produced, and any surplus is then transported to an external warehouse.

When preparing the transport planning, the planner assesses the stock levels of materials at the on-site warehouse and at the external warehouse. If there are sufficient quantities of materials at the external warehouse to fulfil the delivery, it will be dispatched from there.

 System reflection of the process:

We are working on a brownfield implementation with existing sales and planning processes that cannot be changed on the fly. We are in ASR context.

Contracts have been concluded with customers, and the aforementioned recurring delivery requests are recorded in sales via Sales Agreements. When a customer requests a new delivery, a new schedule line is created in the Sales Agreement.


This part of the process already exists and cannot be changed at present.

Another point: each sales agreement also implies the delivery of only one material. By default, a schedule line in a Sales Agreement is assigned to a shipping point. In our case, this is the shipping point of our factory rather than an external warehouse.
Consequently, after a schedule line is created, it is integrated into the Transport Management (TM) module, where the freight unit inherits the shipping point from the schedule line.
An outbound delivery will be created from the TM module, incorporating the material quantity, shipping point, and consignee. 
The challenge we faced: we would like to be able to change the shipping point during transport planning to dispatch goods from a different origin location. -> an external warehouse in our example. Ideally, using standard functions, but personally, I have little faith that this is possible.

What we have already tried and what can be excluded:

1. Creating deliveries from LE and changing the shipping point there, before distribution to TM. Copy control between SA and Delivery does not allow this.
2. Changing the Sales Agreement via the transport planner is not permitted by organisational policies.
3. Creating individual sales orders with a specified shipping point is also not permitted for organisational reasons.

We will most likely have to develop a custom solution, but I decided to seek advice to see if there might be a less obvious solution to the problem. Thank you for your replies!
